purpose: TRUE-fp64 re-confirmation of the +1h/+3h coupled GPU forecast skill vs corpus CPU-WRF, AFTER the Sprint coupler-fp64 fixes (FIX #1 true-fp64 physics adapters; FIX #2 WRF held-rate radiation).
scope: single-case GRIDDED preview of M19 (NOT station-masked TOST); whole-grid T2/U10/V10 RMSE/bias vs CPU-WRF wrfout_d02 at the same valid time.
entry: segmented (host-loop, bounded peak memory; byte-equivalent skill to the production while-loop per segscan_equiv.json -- the two differ only in RRTMG cadence gating jax.lax.cond vs static-bool). Used because the GPU was shared with a concurrent agent and the single-graph production entry OOM'd on a transient (10.65 GiB) under the 0.35 mem-fraction cap; the segmented path bounds peak memory.

config.radiation_cadence_model: WRF held-rate RTHRATEN: recomputed once per radt interval, applied every dynamics step (FIX #2; proofs/precision/radiation_cadence_trace.json)

fp32_contamination_note: The PRIOR (pre-fix) numbers were run with namelist force_fp64=True, but the physics adapters silently cast theta/qv/u/v/qke/hydrometeors back to the frozen fp32 ADR-007 perf matrix INSIDE each timestep, so the physics tendencies were computed/stored in fp32. The end-of-step re-upcast only re-widened already-truncated values. These NEW numbers are the FIRST genuinely-fp64 physics-coupled measurements (verified by the dtype audit).
verdict: TRUE-fp64 skill is essentially UNCHANGED vs the old fp32-contaminated numbers (all |delta RMSE| <= 0.02 K / 0.02 m/s at both +1h and +3h). HONEST FINDING: the fp32 physics-adapter contamination did NOT materially bias the +1h/+3h surface skill -- but the baseline is now genuinely fp64 (dtype-audited) and trustworthy, rather than accidentally-fp32. The forecast remains finite and holds skill to +3h. V10 remains the weak field (RMSE 2.76-3.69), consistent with the surface/PBL momentum-coupling diagnosis (sfclayrev vs sf_mynn), unrelated to the fp64 fix.
